front: Film: elderly cleaning woman Emmi marries Moroccan guest worker Ali; son kicks in TV (homage to Douglas Sirk's All That Heaven Allows); outdoor cafe scene with alienating staff; Fassbinder plays bitter son-in-law Eugen; final shot: Ali in hospital with stomach ulcer
back: Ali: Fear Eats the Soul (Rainer Werner Fassbinder, 1974)

front: Film: bedroom dominated by reproduction of Poussin's Midas and Bacchus; Petra lures Karin as fashion model; silent assistant Marlene packs suitcase and leaves at the end; Marlene and Petra dance to 'Smoke Gets In Your Eyes'; inspired the Maloja Snake play in Assayas's Clouds of Sils Maria
back: The Bitter Tears of Petra von Kant (Rainer Werner Fassbinder, 1972)
work: The Bitter Tears of Petra von Kant (1972)

front: Film: Maria kills African-American soldier Bill; husband Hermann takes the blame; Maria becomes mistress of industrialist Karl Oswald; ends with gas explosion; first of BRD Trilogy (also Lola and Veronika Voss); Lola adapts same source as The Blue Angel
back: The Marriage of Maria Braun (Rainer Werner Fassbinder, 1979)
work: The Marriage of Maria Braun (1979) and BRD Trilogy

front: Film: 15-hour TV miniseries based on Alfred Doblin's novel; Franz Biberkopf released from Tegel prison in 1928 ('The Punishment Begins'); loses arm when Reinhold pushes him from getaway car; Barbara Sukowa plays murdered Mieze; epilogue: atom bomb + Glenn Miller's 'In the Mood' + Franz on crucifix

Director: bisexual West German director; died at 37 having made 40+ films; signed Oberhausen Manifesto ('Papa's cinema is dead'); New German Cinema; heavily influenced by Douglas Sirk's melodramas; final film Querelle (Jean Genet); Tom Stoppard adapted Despair (Nabokov) for his first English film
work: Career, Style, and New German Cinema
